title: How to Make Holiday Gatherings Safer: 6 Things to Keep in Mind
summary: Last year’s holiday season occurred during a raging pandemic and no vaccines in our toolbox. This year’s holiday season has two clear advantages: (1) we have safe and effective vaccines against COVID-19 and (2) we’re much more attuned to risk reduction measures like the availability of at-home testing, ventilation, and better masks that can make any type of gathering safer.
body: Last year’s holiday season occurred during a raging pandemic and no vaccines in our toolbox. This year’s holiday season has two clear advantages: (1) we have safe and effective vaccines against COVID-19 and (2) we’re much more attuned to risk reduction measures like the availability of at-home testing, ventilation, and better masks that can make any type of gathering safer. While it can be tricky to try to calibrate individual and family risk given lots of variability in situations like living with someone who is immunocompromised, unvaccinated children or in an area with substantial community transmission, it is clear we can make any gathering safer by applying risk reduction measures. Now, with the 2021 holiday season approaching, we’re all looking forward to Halloween trick or treating, that Thanksgiving meal with loved ones, Christmas and Hanukah get-togethers and other holiday celebrations. Here are 6 things to keep in mind during your get together. result.main_content_en: The 2021 holiday season presents significant advantages over the previous year, primarily due to the availability of safe and effective COVID-19 vaccines and a greater understanding of risk reduction measures. These measures include at-home testing, improved ventilation, and better-quality masks, which collectively can make gatherings safer. While individual risk assessment remains complex, especially for those living with immunocompromised individuals, unvaccinated children, or in areas of high community transmission, applying layered mitigation strategies is crucial. The article outlines six key considerations for safer holiday celebrations, emphasizing that proactive steps can significantly reduce transmission risks during family and social events like Halloween, Thanksgiving, Christmas, and Hanukkah. The guidance builds on lessons learned throughout the pandemic to help navigate gatherings with greater safety.
result.main_content_zh: 与上一年相比，2021年的假日季具有显著优势，主要归功于安全有效的新冠疫苗的普及以及对风险降低措施的更深入理解。这些措施包括家庭自测、改善通风以及使用更高质量的口罩，这些都能使聚会更加安全。尽管个人风险评估仍然复杂，特别是对于那些与免疫力低下者同住、家中有未接种疫苗的儿童或生活在社区传播严重地区的人们，但采取分层缓解策略至关重要。文章概述了确保假日庆祝活动更安全的六个关键考虑因素，强调积极主动的措施可以显著降低万圣节、感恩节、圣诞节和光明节等家庭及社交活动期间的传播风险。该指南基于整个疫情期间积累的经验教训，旨在帮助人们在聚会中实现更高的安全性。
